alignment_to_round2_review.round2_recommendation: NEW callout #1 (round-2 designer review v2): 'How to Evaluate Any Scientific Claim' preface promotion to Overview as 5-question preface block, each question linking to the section in the review that demonstrates it.
alignment_to_round2_review.round1_finding_addressed: F2 (round-1 brief): give the reader a methodology BEFORE plunging into 71 WINs. The thesis-hero (EXP-255) gives the verdict; the evaluate-preface (EXP-257) gives the method. Together they answer 'what's the answer' and 'how do I check' on the first viewport.
alignment_to_round2_review.operator_directive: HNOTE-007 Item 4 — 5-short-questions, distilled from existing prose, each linked to the demonstrating section. Place above thesis as eyebrow OR below thesis at body weight (analyst's call). EXP-257 chose BELOW at body weight per hero composition reasoning.

final_overview_stack_order_after_all_thr: EXP-256 falsifiability-module — 'What would change our verdict' 4-row block
EXP-255 thesis-hero — display-scale thesis + eclipse 'Live test pending' badge + 4-figure body row
EXP-257 evaluate-preface — 5-question methodology Q&A with section-shortcut labels
accuracy-math-section (existing — Honest accuracy + headline string note)
hero_composition_summary.visual_differentiation_check: All three structured blocks (EXP-256, EXP-255, EXP-257) are visually distinct. EXP-256 uses --accent left rail + grid layout + status badges (verdict-encoded). EXP-255 uses display-scale serif + accent eclipse badge + body figure row (the climax). EXP-257 uses --ink-3 left rail + numbered Q&A + right-aligned section labels (utility/methodology). No risk of pattern-matching the three as 'more of the same'.
hero_composition_summary.above_the_fold_estimate_post_three_exps: On 1440x900 desktop: title-block ~140px + verdict bar ~150px + EXP-256 falsifiability-module ~280px + EXP-255 thesis-hero ~350px ≈ 920px. Thesis-hero remains visible-or-just-at-fold. EXP-257 evaluate-preface (~210px estimated) starts just below the fold; reader scrolls one screen to see it. This is the correct hierarchy: thesis is the headline, methodology is the 'and here's how' that follows.

audit_finding_summary.eg_evaluate_canonical_questions_verbatim: All 5 questions reused verbatim from existing eg-evaluate (docs/index.html line 482, in tab 'evaluate'). Source TLDR quote: 'Five questions to ask of any claim — from the dome model or from us: Does the prediction distinguish this model from alternatives? Can it be derived from the model's own parameters? Is the data accurately represented? Is the claim falsifiable? Are the same data counted multiple times?'
audit_finding_summary.no_invented_questions: Verified — no question 6, no rewording of any of the 5. The HNOTE 'no invented conditions' / 'no derived content' rule is honored.
audit_finding_summary.medicine_analogy_unchanged: The medicine-analogy long-form treatment in eg-evaluate stays in the Evaluation Guide tab unchanged. EXP-257 preface caption links to it ('Full preface →') so discoverability of the analogy goes UP, not down.

five_link_targets_resolved.q1_distinguish_alternatives: → #stdmodel (3.4 Detailed: Standard Model Explains, tab 'wins') — canonical bucket for non-discriminating claims
five_link_targets_resolved.q2_derive_from_own_params: → #selfcon (3.3 Detailed: Self-Contradicted, tab 'wins') — canonical bucket for WINs whose dome-derived value contradicts the dome's own claim
five_link_targets_resolved.q3_data_accurately_represented: → #refuted (3.2 Detailed: Refuted by Data, tab 'wins') — canonical bucket for cited data that doesn't support the claim
five_link_targets_resolved.q4_claim_falsifiable: → #misleading (3.6 Detailed: Misleading and Unfalsifiable, tab 'wins') — combined bucket including 4 explicit Unfalsifiable verdicts (aetheric refraction, etc.)
five_link_targets_resolved.q5_same_data_counted_multiple_times: → #dedup-analysis (6.6b De-Duplication Analysis, tab 'predictions') — explicit conservative-clustering treatment showing 71 → ~40 independent claims after de-duplication

alternative_link_targets_considered_and_.q1_alt: killshots (Part 5 Kill-Shot Binary Tests) — rejected because focused on 6 specific binary tests, narrower than the broader non-discrimination pattern the question detects
alternative_link_targets_considered_and_.q4_alt: eg-falsifiability (What Would Change Our Verdict) — rejected because it's about OUR review's falsifiability, not the dome's. Question 4 is asking about evaluating the DOME's claims, so it links to the dome-claim bucket. Also: eg-falsifiability is already linked twice (EXP-256 falsifiability-module caption + EXP-257 preface caption), avoiding redirect collision.
alternative_link_targets_considered_and_.q5_alt: 2-2-tidal-pattern-one-spike-vs-two-bulges (the canonical tidal-cluster example) — rejected as too narrow (specific to tidal physics). 6.6b De-Duplication Analysis is the structural treatment that generalizes.

summary: EXP-257 inserts a 5-question methodology preface on the Overview tab, BELOW the EXP-255 thesis-hero and ABOVE the accuracy-math-section. Each of the 5 questions (verbatim from eg-evaluate) is a link to the verdict bucket that demonstrates the corresponding failure mode (questions 1-4 → 3.4/3.3/3.2/3.6 in tab 'wins'; question 5 → 6.6b in tab 'predictions'). Caption links back to the full eg-evaluate medicine-analogy treatment. Two patch segments (HTML insert + CSS rule), both anchors verified unique and robust against EXP-255 / EXP-256 integration order. Build dry-run passed (+0.3% file size); tests passed (3454 vs 3452 baseline, +2 from new internal-link checks). Routed for curmudgeon adversarial review per HNOTE explicit directive (NOT auto-integrate). Completes the EXP-255 / EXP-256 / EXP-257 trio for HNOTE-OPERATOR-DESIGN-REVIEW-V2-OVERVIEW-REWORK-001 — once all three integrate, HNOTE flips from pending to consumed.
